purpose: Claude Code skill definition for `eager-dispatch`. It describes trigger conditions, workflow, constraints, and referenced protocol files for that skill. Key content: --- name: eager-dispatch description: > Run a standalone eager dispatch round on an active Synapse swarm. Reads current dashboard state, identifies all tasks whose dependencies are satisfied but haven't been dispatched, and dispatches them ALL immediately with full worker prompts. Use for recovering from stalls, manual intervention after pauses, or ensuring no dispatchable tasks are sitting idle. argument-hint: "[--dashboard <id>]" user-invocable The file is 226 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
patterns: Skill-definition pattern: trigger guidance first, then workflow steps and referenced protocol files; these files are loaded on demand by Claude Code.
Uses explicit NON-NEGOTIABLE language to encode hard behavioral constraints for agents.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
